header.headline: v6.6 Edge-Case Matrix
header.subtitle: Cases that should stay explicit to keep future operator behavior predictable.

legend.title: Legend
legend.note: Rows pair each explicit case with the minimum surviving check and the owning surface.
legend.warning: Highlight rows are the ones most likely to create false release confidence if they drift silently.

groups.title: Gate and Runtime Cases
groups.caption: Cases that directly change gate truth values or runtime correctness.
rows: SKIP rows can still fail strict require-all gates
Build and smoke confidence should survive network outages
Window drift can silently break decode parity

groups.title: Layout and Stability Cases
groups.caption: Schema, wrapper, and long-run failures that create brittle operator behavior.
rows: Schema drift breaks smoke and analysis parsers
Default-cache assumptions create false confidence in local runs
Short smoke can pass while sustained decode diverges
Shared cache writes can create nondeterministic failures
run v6.6-gate in isolated output dirs

footer.title: Maintenance Rule
footer.body: Update the matrix whenever gate commands, require-all behavior, or report schemas change.
